title: OpenAI plans $20 billion data center campus near Savannah, Georgia
excerpt: The project, called Project Camellia, will draw 3.2 gigawatts of power from Georgia Power in phases between 2028 and 2032
content_html:
Cris Tolomia

Wed, July 22, 2026 at 10:28 AM PDT 3 min read

OpenAI announced plans Wednesday to build a data center campus in Effingham County, Georgia, committing $20 billion to the project in order to qualify for a local incentive package. The facility, named Project Camellia, is located within the Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub, about 45 minutes outside of Savannah, and would be the first data center that OpenAI is designing and building itself.

The company has contracted with Georgia Power to receive 3.2 gigawatts of power, with delivery expected in phases between 2028 and 2032. At the full scale of the project, total costs would likely exceed $30 billion, according to Bloomberg. OpenAI is currently seeking partners to help finance and build the campus, the company said.

Under a 25-year agreement, OpenAI has committed to providing up to 1,000 megawatts of flexible demand response, Georgia Power said. That means OpenAI will reduce its power consumption during periods of peak grid demand before residential customers are affected. Georgia Power said the arrangement is among the largest single-facility demand response commitments in the country.

OpenAI said electricity rates for existing Georgia Power customers will not increase because of the project, and that it will pay the full cost of electrical infrastructure and service. The data center will use a closed-loop cooling system designed to keep ongoing water use low, the company said.

On the community side, OpenAI said it has pledged $80 million in community benefits across the life of the project, targeting areas including education, public safety, health care, workforce development, housing, and local small business support. OpenAI also anticipates its tax obligations to the state and county will reach into the hundreds of millions of dollars, which it said would make it Effingham County's single largest taxpayer. An additional $71 million in credits for OpenAI's Codex coding tool will be made available to eligible students at Georgia colleges and technical schools. In a statement cited by Axios, Effingham County Commissioner Jamie DeLoach said the project's low water demands, its lack of impact on residential energy bills, and the new tax revenue it would bring were among the factors that won over county officials. The county projects that revenue flowing from the facility could cut property tax bills for a typical homeowner by roughly 40%.

OpenAI plans to hold a public open house on Thursday where residents can meet the project team and provide input that will shape a formal community compact. The company also said it will commission an annual independent audit to verify compliance with its commitments.

content_html_zh:
克里斯·托洛米亚

太平洋夏令时间 2026 年 7 月 22 日星期三上午 10:28 阅读时间 3 分钟

OpenAI 周三宣布计划在佐治亚州埃芬汉县建设一个数据中心园区，并承诺为该项目投资 200 亿美元，以获得当地的激励计划。 该设施名为 Project Camellia，位于 Savannah Gateway 工业中心内，距萨凡纳约 45 分钟路程，将是 OpenAI 自行设计和建造的第一个数据中心。

该公司已与佐治亚电力公司签订了接收 3.2 吉瓦电力的合同，预计在 2028 年至 2032 年期间分阶段交付。 据彭博社报道，该项目全面启动后，总成本可能会超过 300 亿美元。 该公司表示，OpenAI 目前正在寻找合作伙伴来帮助融资和建设园区。

乔治亚电力公司表示，根据一项为期 25 年的协议，OpenAI 承诺提供高达 1,000 兆瓦的灵活需求响应。 这意味着 OpenAI 将在住宅客户受到影响之前，在电网需求高峰期间减少电力消耗。 乔治亚电力公司表示，该安排是该国最大的单一设施需求响应承诺之一。

OpenAI 表示，乔治亚电力公司现有客户的电费不会因为该项目而增加，并且将支付电力基础设施和服务的全部成本。 该公司表示，该数据中心将使用闭环冷却系统，旨在保持较低的用水量。

在社区方面，OpenAI 表示已承诺在项目整个生命周期内提供 8000 万美元的社区福利，目标领域包括教育、公共安全、医疗保健、劳动力发展、住房和当地小企业支持。 OpenAI 还预计其对州和县的纳税义务将达到数亿美元，并表示这将使其成为埃芬汉县最大的单一纳税人。 OpenAI Codex 编码工具的额外 7100 万美元学分将提供给佐治亚州学院和技术学校符合条件的学生。 在 Axios 引用的一份声明中，埃芬汉县专员杰米·德洛奇 (Jamie DeLoach) 表示，该项目的用水需求低、对住宅能源账单影响不大，以及它将带来新的税收收入，这些都是赢得县官员支持的因素。 该县预计，该设施产生的收入可以使普通房主的财产税减少约 40%。

OpenAI 计划于周四举行公众开放日，居民可以与项目团队会面，并提供意见，以形成正式的社区契约。 该公司还表示，将委托进行年度独立审计，以核实其承诺的遵守情况。
